mnemonics: Think of concatenation like linking two train cars together. The engine (first list) pulls the caboose (second list) to form one long train.
keyPoints: Concatenation combines two lists end-to-end to create a new, longer list.
The original lists remain unchanged; the operation produces a new list as a result.
The order of elements is preserved, with all elements from the first list followed by all elements from the second.
commonMistakes: Modifying the original lists expecting them to change (they don't; concatenation is not an in-place operation).
Confusing concatenation (joining end-to-end) with merging (combining elements based on a condition or key).
